ci_support_linux_64_r_base4.4.yaml:
channel_sources:
- conda-forge
channel_targets:
- conda-forge main
cran_mirror:
- https://cran.r-project.org
docker_image:
- quay.io/condaforge/linux-anvil-x86_64:alma9
pin_run_as_build:
  r-base:
    min_pin: x.x
    max_pin: x.x
r_base:
- '4.4'
target_platform:
- linux-64

about.summary: Seven different methods for multiple testing problems. The SGoF-type methods include, for example, Carvajal Rodriguez et al. (2009) <doi:10.1186/1471-2105-10-209>, de Una Alvarez (2012) <doi:10.1515/1544-6115.1812>, and Castro Conde et al. (2015) <doi:10.1177/0962280215597580>, along with the BH and BY false discovery rate controlling procedures.

Seven different methods for multiple testing problems. The SGoF-type methods include, for example, Carvajal Rodriguez et al. (2009) <doi:10.1186/1471-2105-10-209>, de Una Alvarez (2012) <doi:10.1515/1544-6115.1812>, and Castro Conde et al. (2015) <doi:10.1177/0962280215597580>, along with the BH and BY false discovery rate controlling procedures.

name: r-sgof
raw_meta_yaml:
{% set version = '2.3.5' %}
{% set posix = 'm2-' if win else '' %}

package:
  name: r-sgof
  version: "{{ version|replace('-', '_') }}"

source:
  url:
    - {{ cran_mirror }}/src/contrib/sgof_{{ version }}.tar.gz
    - {{ cran_mirror }}/src/contrib/Archive/sgof/sgof_{{ version }}.tar.gz
  sha256: 4084457e0b09ccf4da2568b195149832837eaa12e3825c3837cf658982560f09

build:
  number: 0
  noarch: generic
  rpaths:
    - lib/R/lib/
    - lib/
  missing_dso_whitelist:
    - '*/R.dll'        # [win]
    - '*/Rblas.dll'    # [win]
    - '*/Rlapack.dll'  # [win]

requirements:
  build:
    - {{ posix }}zip               # [win]
    - cross-r-base {{ r_base }}    # [build_platform != target_platform]
  host:
    - r-base
    - r-poibin
  run:
    - r-base
    - r-poibin

test:
  commands:
    - $R -e "library('sgof')"           # [not win]
    - "\"%R%\" -e \"library('sgof')\""  # [win]

about:
  home: https://CRAN.R-project.org/package=sgof
  license: GPL-2.0-only
  summary: Seven different methods for multiple testing problems. The SGoF-type methods
    include, for example, Carvajal Rodriguez et al. (2009) <doi:10.1186/1471-2105-10-209>,
    de Una Alvarez (2012) <doi:10.1515/1544-6115.1812>, and Castro Conde et al.
    (2015) <doi:10.1177/0962280215597580>, along with the BH and BY false discovery
    rate controlling procedures.
  license_family: GPL2
  license_file:
    - '{{ environ["PREFIX"] }}/lib/R/share/licenses/GPL-2'

extra:
  recipe-maintainers:
    - conda-forge/r

# Package: sgof
# Type: Package
# Encoding: UTF-8
# Title: Multiple Hypothesis Testing
# Version: 2.3.5
# Date: 2024-07-10
# Author: Irene Castro Conde and Jacobo de Una Alvarez
# Maintainer: Irene Castro Conde <irene.castro@uvigo.es>
# Depends: R (>= 3.6), stats, poibin
# Suggests: DiscreteDatasets
# Description: Seven different methods for multiple testing problems. The SGoF-type methods (see for example, Carvajal Rodriguez et al., 2009 <doi:10.1186/1471-2105-10-209>;  de Una Alvarez, 2012 <doi:10.1515/1544-6115.1812>; Castro Conde et al., 2015 <doi:10.1177/0962280215597580>) and the BH and BY false discovery rate controlling procedures.
# License: GPL-2
# LazyData: yes
# NeedsCompilation: no
# Packaged: 2024-07-10 09:03:33 UTC; icastro
# Repository: CRAN
# Date/Publication: 2024-07-17 15:10:02 UTC
